name: What Arlen Found
description: Since the Day, Arlen has been mapping the resonance connections in the Foundation network -- not out of academic interest, but because he has found something specific. For eleven years he extended resonance toward the sealed mines on the edge of the Wastes, not knowing why. What he found in the network is that the extension was two-directional. Someone else was extending from inside the mines. He knows who it was.
accept_text: He is in the Hive hall, the one that has been receiving visitors since the Day, and he is not receiving anyone at the moment. He is at a table with a small map of resonance connection signatures, the kind of technical notation that the network makes legible only from inside. He folds it when he sees you. 'I told you something once, when we first spoke about the eleven years,' he says. 'I told you I had been extending resonance toward the sealed mines. I didn't know why. It was compulsive -- the kind of thing you do for years before you understand that you have been doing it.' He sets the map down. 'In the network, I can see the resonance history. The signature of a connection -- how it was initiated, how it developed, what it reached toward.' He looks at the map. 'For eleven years I was reaching. And something was reaching back. I thought for a long time it was the crystal seam itself -- crystal resonates, that's what crystal does. But the signature of what was reaching back from the mines is not a crystal signature.' He pauses. 'It is a human signature. Someone in those mines, inside the crystal seam, extending resonance outward for eleven years without knowing they were doing it.' He looks at you. 'The signature matches Rund. Who has been in that seam for twenty years.' He folds his hands. 'He doesn't know. I want him to know. I don't know how to reach him directly through the network -- the mines are deep, the crystal seam creates its own resonance environment, and he hasn't established a stable connection point yet.' He looks at you. 'Go tell him. Tell him that for eleven years, we were reaching toward each other without knowing. And that it worked.'
complete_text: Rund is sitting with his back against the crystal wall. He does not move when you come in -- he is in the particular stillness of someone who has spent twenty years learning to be still in a crystal seam, the kind of stillness that is not absence but presence. He listens to everything you say without interrupting. When you finish, he is quiet for a long time. The crystal in the wall behind him is doing something you have seen it do once before -- the specific pulse that you have come to associate with the Grid's construction frequency, patient and even. 'For eleven years,' he says finally. Not a question. 'Eleven years.' He puts his hand on the crystal wall. 'I have been in here for twenty years. The first nine years I thought I was learning the crystal. I thought the way the seam responded to me was just -- physics. Material properties. The way things work in a resonant environment.' He looks at his hand on the wall. 'The last eleven years I thought I was talking to the seam. I thought that was the extent of it -- me and the crystal, finding a language.' He is quiet. 'I was talking to a person.' He looks at you. 'I didn't know there was a person. I didn't know someone was on the other end.' He takes his hand off the wall slowly. 'Tell him.' He pauses. 'Tell him I knew something was there. I didn't know what. I just kept talking because the silence in here is different when you're talking into it.' He looks at the wall. 'Tell him I'm going to keep talking. Now that I know what I'm talking to.'
